message:
fix(scheduler): prevent priority inversion by checking cancel/timed before ready lanes

The three-lane scheduler's dispatch loop previously checked fast ready
paths (fast_queue + local_ready) in Phase 2 before checking cancel and
timed lanes in Phase 3 via try_local_all_lanes(). This ordering allowed
a steady stream of ready tasks to indefinitely starve pending
cancellations and timed tasks — a classic priority inversion.

This commit reorders the dispatch phases:
- Phase 2 (new): Check local PriorityScheduler cancel and timed lanes
  first, using the renamed try_local_priority_lanes() which only handles
  cancel (lane 0) and timed (lane 1) — no longer includes ready
- Phase 3: Fast ready paths (fast_queue + local_ready) — unchanged logic
- Phase 3b (new): Dedicated local ready lane pop with its own rng_hint,
  separated from the priority lanes to make the scheduling contract
  explicit

The try_local_all_lanes() method is renamed to try_local_priority_lanes()
and its return type simplified to only emit lane tags 0 (cancel) and
1 (timed), removing the ready-lane fallback that previously lived inside
the same lock acquisition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
